item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An amazing concept to wrap your head around but also frightening in the sense history could be rewritten with just one 1237-1 taking a quick nap.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A little similar to 231 since the Foundation is doing (presumably, if you don't headcanon Fear Alone) terrible things to prevent the end of the world, like ethnic cleansing. This is very good.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my thoughts precisely

it kinda works on a meta level, too, just like that one 001

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Class Omega is a little cheesy and over the top, in my opinion. I'd say replace it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus. This really captures the spirit of the foundation perfectly. Forget Procedure 110-Montauk, this is the Foundation promoting freaking ethnic cleansing. While usually annoyed by longer containment procedures, in this case it actually got the level of foreboding in the scp off the charts. Extremely well done, and easily one of my new favorite scps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my favourites. Definitely ties up a lot of things in my headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. I think this is an example where the Special Containment Procedures work towards generating interest/building up tension: in this case, one wonders what can possibly be so bad that the Foundation is willing to support genocide for its containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off

Whereas SCP-1237-1-negative individuals do not exhibit REM activity during the deepest stages of sleep, SCP-1237-1-positives do

Why can't we just call them SCP-1237-1-N and SCP-1237-1-P?

To be honest, before I read the addendum, i was gonna give a neutral vote. I liked the idea, but just reading that whole section would give me a strong no vote. Reading the addendum got me really interested though. Giving the idea that dreams really do come true *rimshot* But, the addendum gave me a really weak up vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if they dream there is a virus that infects everyone but only kills people with the gene?

item.title: No difference to me. . .
item.content:encoded: Whether or not you yourself were influenced by The Lathe of Heaven in writing this makes no difference to me, and in fact just makes me more impressed with you for coming up with this concept on your own. The fact of the matter is that The Lathe of Heaven is one of my favorite books ever, I love hte underlying concept of dreams which change reality, and therefore I love this SCP of yours as well. It gets a VERY enthusiastic upvote on my part :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sounds similar to SCP-781 in the "dreams become reality" idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that SCP-1237 events can be controlled by SCP-781, as he controlled the old version of SCP-122.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, sweet, and scary. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just came across this one. Well written. And Lathe was my first thought as well. Chasing down these individuals would be difficult, particularly if they are aware of the Foundation and don't want to be caught.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Smapti, the ideas you come up with are nothing short of amazing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love these really ambitious scips.

This is also a really good example of how to do a 682 cross-test and not have it be retarded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder what would happen if it was discovered that SCP-239 was affected by SCP-1237 during her coma?
meh im too lazy to do a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…sponsorship of organizations participating in campaigns of involuntary sterilization, ethnic cleansing, or genocide is authorized…

Yikes.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Happy to see I wasn't the only one who got that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted just on the basis of that clever wordplay right at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has research been done to determine whether it's hereditary or simply a spontaneous genetic mutation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's 'knows of' rather than 'knows personally'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I usually skip containment procedures until the end, but here they do a marvelous job of setting up the rest of the piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy…

"The Origin" SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If that's true, how could governments switch politico-ideological bases due to SCP-1237? If the dreams only affect those the dreamer knows, wouldn't that invalidate certain effects listed in the article? Or am I missing something here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm assuming that the class-Ω amnesiac thing means that every single bit of the person's memories from birth were obliterated.

Overall, one of the better posts in the 1000s, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I can't get it out of my mind that 682 was the result of some horrid nightmare, involving 682 being impossible to destroy D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think that would quite work the way i've written this. The dreams can only affect people the dreamer knows, so at most the dreamer could remove the gene from any positives s/he was introduced to - and if they're in Foundation custody, then they're already mind-wiped vegetables on drugs who are no threat to anyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if this could be a self-eradicating SCP without causing some sort of paradox by using 1237-1-L to dream a world where 1237 does not exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts exactly. It doesn't read as a rip-off, though — the concept of "effective dreams" is nebulous enough that I definitely believe this was generated independently.

And quite independent of all that, too, I like this article. The extremity of the containment procedures struck a frightening chord with me, as did the idea of "how many times have these people already pulled the rug out from under reality?" +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also don't forget that not every change is going to be some catastrophic, world-changing thing. Did you ever go to bed at night putting your (cell phone / car keys / wallet / whatever) on your nightstand, only to wake up and find them still in your jacket pocket?

The obvious, logical solution is that you just forgot that time but "remembered" the habit… The other explanation is that you had a very, very mundane dream last night..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, because I feel that it does well that which is so often done poorly.

It's not that humanoids, reality benders, Keter-class, lethal things are bad, but the reason that people are encouraged not to start with them is that there are so many of them done badly, and the those are done so frequently.

One that is well written, properly presented, and actually scary? That deserves an upvote.

Does this need to be 001? No, but if it was written and presented that way I would still vote for it.

One last note, even with 70 million dreamers, it would make sense that the world hasn't ended (yet). First, since there is a fundamental restructuring, we wouldn't know if what we define as reality were changed. Secondly, with only one event for the overwhelming majority, without a way to control when the change would be much more likely to be something small in terms of the entire world. In other words, many marriages to childhood sweethearts, unexpected one night stands with unlikely partners, and small injustices rectified. Few Palin/Clinton presidents for life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
False. We have always been at war with Eastasia.

I like this for everything except what Vezaz said. I'd suggest toning it down a little?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry, but I was never a fan of the reality benders—I think they're kind of boring, and now you've gone and mass produced them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I should probably touch it up a bitn to explain later, but the way I see it working is that memories are "semi-affected".

Looking at the first post, imagine that X dreamed that Y was president. Y would know he was president, and all the paperwork would say Y was president, and people would hear Y's name and know he was president. But they'd still remember, in some lingering sense, that Z was president before - in the same sense that things can change in the middle of a dream and you just accept it.

In-universe, this could explain a lot of conflicts that arise over disputes regarding easily verifiable facts. Maybe a given conspiracy theory was true until someone dreamed it was the work of a lone nut?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there something in there that says the memory of observers is affected? I looked through a couple times and couldn't find any mention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keep one captive long enough and it'll dream of the Foundation's nonexistence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just trying to keep my thoughts on this thing in one place. I may have gotten a bit off-topic;.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sup, Anaxa. This isn't the 682 log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't much care for it.

This doesn't seem at all stoppable. It's just too widespread and too powerful. So many of these people are already out there that one of them is guaranteed going to dream something that renders the current world into something too utterly different for the Foundation to exist in. Also, the 682 cross-test was unnecessary, and suffered from the thing where a creature that is at least somewhat vulnerable to small-arms fire and a fairly weak acid is immune to EVERYTHING EVER, even where it makes no logical sense for it to be. Seriously, 682 has become just excessively overpowered. I'd love to see that log pruned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What makes you think things haven't been changing? Haven't you ever noticed how in dreams, even the strangest, most illogical things are simply accepted by everyone in the dream?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keep in mind, I don't like anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm flattered that so many people think this could be SCP-001, but Bright seems unimpressed so that probably won't happen. :p I don't want to be so bold as to say "This is where SCPs come from" or "This is why the Foundation came to be", though the latter might be fuel for a Tale or something. I think most SCPs are better when their origin isn't known, so I leave it to the reader's imagination whether any particular SCP had anything to do with this one. I might write an article eventually about an SCP specifically created by a dreamer - I don't know yet.

Re Lathe of Heaven: I've read some of Leguin's other books but not that one, and I didn't know it was about reality-bending dreams. Having read the Wikipedia article on it id say mine differs in that the dreamers have no control over when they have these dreams or what they're about, only things they've encountered in person are affected, and the rest of the world can remember the way things were originally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why black out the zero then? It implies, to me at least, that the percentage is one or higher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's why I took the number out to three decimal places - it's probably closer to 70,000 than 70 million. Many of them go their entire lives without having an incident, and the ones that do rarely have more than one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even at 1% of the total population, that's still 70 million people. I have a hard time believing that things would not be changing daily, and noticeably.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While this is well written and the idea itself is not necessarily bad, I almost feel this is too threatening to just be an SCP article. Personally, I feel a concept of this scope should be treated differently, and there isn't a significant twist/hook to make it work for me as a regular SCP. I'd kind of like to see this as a 001 proposal, as others have said. Perhaps the Foundation started off as a collection unaffiliated organizations, all dedicated towards stopping the dreamers. Over the years, they became aware of each other and formed what became to be the Foundation, and started containing other anomalies once it became a problem. Perhaps one of the founders ends up coming forth and revealing that his child had dreamt of such an organization shortly before the Foundation's establishment, akin to your idea in the thread this was originally posted in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enh. Not really that interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw your post about this on the forum, but didn't read the sandbox article. You pulled this off wonderfully. All the wide-reaching implications are well explored in the containment procedures. The scale of this is incredible. What works most for me is that nothing external causes this to happen - it's just something that can happen in the DNA of the population. That is 1000x more terrifying than a dream ray or something that mutates people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the addendum is driving the point home a bit too much; you had me with "legal or otherwise". But wow, this could explain so many SCPs in itself. That's really amazing, and the drive to stop it is terrifying. That's a successful SCP if you ask me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirding. This could certainly be the origin of many, if not all SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Lathe of Heaven made into an SCP? Never thought I'd live to see the day, it's such a little-known book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's what I was thinking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now.. you changed the last note from O5 to a senior researcher which was my only complaint which was my only beef with this…and then I seen what you did with lucid dreamers and read the addition to 682's log which is made of win, IMHO.
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could be a SCP-001, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting-a genetic etiology for reality benders. The containment is a little long, and the addendum a bit heavy-handed, but I like it overall.

I knew Al Gore won that election…
